generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARPointCloudSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides raw LiDAR point cloud data for object detection and path tracking in the vehicle's proximity. Operates at high frequency (10-30Hz) and supports 2D plane representation processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects real-time vehicle state data including speed, position, orientation, acceleration, and sensor fusion outputs from LiDAR, cameras, and radar."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Monitors motor actuation parameters such as torque, RPM, and throttle commands for real-time feedback to the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Tracks steering servo actuation data including angle, velocity, and position for closed-loop control and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
]

instance ObjectTrackingSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generates structured object tracks from raw LiDAR point clouds using clustering and tracking algorithms (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ObjectTrackDataTransmitted
]

instance PathPlanningSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generates projected driving paths for Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) based on sensor fusion and lead vehicle detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ProjectedPathDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARPointCloudSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance SteeringServoDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance ObjectTrackDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ObjectTrackingSensor
]

instance ProjectedPathDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PathPlanningSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance RawLiDARPointCloudModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Represents raw LiDAR point cloud data in 2D plane format for preprocessing and object detection."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectTrackModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from raw LiDAR point clouds using clustering and tracking algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, position, and orientation for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Generated driving path for ACC based on sensor fusion and lead vehicle detection algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PathVisualizationEnabler
]

instance ACCControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control logic including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and safety logic offloaded to the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw and processed LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from the physical twin to the Digital Twin for object detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudPreprocessingEnabler, ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, sensor inputs, and projected driving path for monitoring and control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor actuation data including torque, RPM, and throttle commands for closed-loop control and feedback."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorActuationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering servo actuation data including angle, velocity, and position for path planning and control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks in the vehicle's proximity for real-time visualization and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, PathVisualizationEnabler
]

instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving path generated by path planning algorithms for ACC and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedPathDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PathVisualizationEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Handles raw LiDAR point cloud preprocessing including noise filtering, outlier removal, and alignment for accurate object detection."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ObjectTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Converts raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object tracks using clustering and tracking algorithms (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables real-time visualization, logging, and reporting of vehicle state data, object tracks, and projected driving paths."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance PathVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Prepares data for 3D visualization including rendering optimization and coordinate transformations for the user interface."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC logic from resource-constrained ECUs to the Digital Twin, enabling distributed computing and closed-loop control delegation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Aligns message types, performs unit conversions, and ensures data serialization for seamless integration across components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ROS2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates modular integration of software components within the Digital Twin using ROS2 inter-node communication."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataStorageAndReportingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Stores processed data and generates reports for analysis, debugging, and compliance."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance LowLatencyNetworkingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Ensures real-time, bidirectional communication between the physical twin and Digital Twin using 5G or dedicated wireless protocols (e.g., IEEE 802.11p) with 100ms end-to-end latency."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance EdgeComputingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Partially processes data at the network edge to reduce cloud dependency and latency for safety-critical applications."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance ACCAlgorithmEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Implements ACC logic including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and safety logic using algorithms like time-to-collision (TTC) or adaptive cruise control models."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization, logging, and reporting of vehicle state data, object tracks, and projected driving paths for operators and developers."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight, HistoricalDataReportingInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC logic from the vehicle's ECUs to the Digital Twin, enabling distributed computing and closed-loop control delegation with high-bandwidth, low-latency communication."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCLogicOffloadingAction, DesiredSpeedComputationInsight, SafetyLogicExecutionInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables teleoperation and safety-critical intervention, allowing operators to remotely enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and issue emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCControlAction, RemoteOperationInsight, EmergencyBrakingOverrideAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Displays real-time vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, and sensor inputs for situational awareness."
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualizes detected object tracks in the vehicle's proximity using sensor fusion from LiDAR, cameras, and radar."
]

instance HistoricalDataReportingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Enables users to view, save, and generate reports of historical vehicle state data for analysis and debugging."
]

instance ACCLogicOffloadingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Offloads the entire ACC system from the vehicle's ECUs to the Digital Twin for distributed computing."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DesiredSpeedComputationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Computes desired speed based on distance to the lead vehicle using algorithms like time-to-collision (TTC) or adaptive cruise control models."
]

instance SafetyLogicExecutionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Executes safety logic for ACC including collision avoidance and emergency braking protocols."
]

instance ACCControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Allows operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and issue control commands remotely."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RemoteOperationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Enables development, testing, and operation of ACC from a location physically separate from the vehicle."
]

instance EmergencyBrakingOverrideAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issues emergency braking commands remotely while maintaining end-to-end latency constraints (100ms)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environment and Components
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hosts resource-specific functionality including sensors, actuators, and general automotive features like Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking."
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manages data and control mechanisms, including ACC logic offloading, closed-loop control, and real-time synchronization with the physical twin."
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Contains the User Interface Functional Entity, split into 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents for interaction with the Digital Twin."
]

instance UserInterfaceFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Provides the user interface for real-time visualization and control, including 3D visualization of vehicle state and object tracks."
]

instance ThreeDVisualizationSubcomponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Renders real-time 3D visualizations of vehicle state, object tracks, and projected driving paths for situational awareness."
]

instance ControlPanelSubcomponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Enables operators to remotely manage ACC, including enabling/disabling ACC, setting desired speeds, and issuing emergency commands."
]

instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Converts raw LiDAR point cloud data into structured object tracks using clustering and tracking algorithms."
]

instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles raw sensor data preprocessing including filtering, normalization, and feature extraction for LiDAR, motor, steering, and vehicle state data."
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Aligns message types, performs unit conversions, and ensures data serialization for seamless integration across components."
]

instance DataStorageAndReportingFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Stores processed data and generates reports for analysis, debugging, and compliance with safety standards."
]

instance ACCLogicFE : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Implements the Adaptive Cruise Control logic including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and safety logic."
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "The decentralized Digital Twin System environment hosting the Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User Entities."
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity, DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Control Flows from Digital to Physical:**
  - **Command Types:**
    - **Actuation Commands:** Motor actuation (e.g., throttle, brake), steering servo actuation (e.g., wheel angle adjustments).
    - **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) Commands:** Speed adjustments, path corrections based on projected driving path and object tracks.
    - **Control Signals:** Generic control signals for vehicle components (e.g., servo control, motor control).

  - **Control Message Formats:**
    - Not explicitly detailed, but inferred to be standardized via the **Data Translation FE**, which aligns message types and ensures compatibility.
    - Likely follows ROS2 message formats for intra-entity communication.

  - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly mentioned in the document. No details on cryptographic authentication or command validation.

  - **Feedback Systems:**
    - **3D Visualization:** Provides real-time feedback (e.g., object tracks, projected path) to the user (e.g., driver or operator).
    - **Reporting FE:** Generates reports for post-hoc analysis, but no real-time feedback loop to the physical system is described.
    - No explicit mention of closed-loop feedback (e.g., sensor confirmation of command execution).

  - **Conflict Resolution:**
    - Not explicitly addressed. Potential conflicts (e.g., conflicting actuation commands) are not described in the document.

  - **Command Execution Confirmation:**
    - Not explicitly mentioned. No acknowledgment or confirmation mechanism is detailed for command execution.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services, but the **100 ms clock synchronization** serves as a baseline for latency requirements across the system, ensuring real-time alignment between the Physical Twin Entity (sensors/actuators) and the Digital Twin Entity (data management/control). No further granularity (e.g., microsecond-level precision) is documented, implying a focus on millisecond-scale synchronization for functional coordination."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **entity-based architectural pattern** rooted in the **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)**, with deviations tailored to automotive use cases. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  1. **Component Integration**: Each FE is encapsulated as a **ROS2 package**, with nodes implementing specific functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* in the Physical Twin Entity). The architecture enforces **modularity** and **loose coupling** between entities via ROS2’s publish-subscribe model.
  2. **Resource Management**: Hardware resources (compute, sensors, actuators) are explicitly tied to the **Physical Twin Entity**, which hosts both resource-specific and general automotive functionalities. This design contrasts with ISO 23247’s *OME*, where resource-specificity is stricter.
  3. **System-Wide Coordination**: The **100 ms clock synchronization** underpins cross-entity coordination, while ROS2’s middleware ensures real-time data exchange. The **User Entity** acts as a centralized interface for visualization and control, abstracting lower-level interactions between the Digital Twin and Physical Twin Entities.
  4. **Deviations from ISO 23247**: The *User Interface FE* is split into subcomponents (*3D Visualization* and *Control Panel*), and the *Data Storage* component lacks a direct FE alignment, requiring custom integration. These deviations impact **ownership of functionality** and the engineering process, as entities are deployed on distinct hardware."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **ISO 23247 standard** does not explicitly detail **model fidelity requirements** or **validation/verification (V&V) methods** for the Digital Twin (DT) system. However, the **entity-based reference architecture** implies that fidelity is ensured through the **accurate digital representation of Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)** by the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)**. Uncertainty sources and quantification are not addressed, though the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** suggests a reliance on real-time data from OMEs, which may introduce **sensor or transmission errors**. Error detection and correction mechanisms are not specified, but the **modular Functional Entities (FEs)** could theoretically implement **cross-validation or anomaly detection** within their designated tasks. Quality assurance processes are not outlined, though the **User Entity**’s role in monitoring data implies a **human-in-the-loop validation** mechanism. The standard does not reference formal **uncertainty quantification techniques** (e.g., Monte Carlo, Bayesian inference) or **error correction protocols** (e.g., Kalman filters, machine learning-based calibration)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Used for intra-entity (software component) communication within the Digital Twin.
    - **Wi-Fi (UDP):** Used for inter-entity communication (Physical Twin → Digital Twin and vice versa) at the transport layer.

  - **Network Topology and Architecture:**
    - **Distributed Architecture:** Physical Twin (sensors/actuators) communicates with the Digital Twin over Wi-Fi.
    - **Modular Design:** Digital Twin components (e.g., Data Translation, Visualization, ACC Controlling FE) communicate via ROS2.
    - No explicit mention of centralized vs. decentralized control or specific network topology (e.g., star, mesh).

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - **Real-Time Constraints:** Implied by real-time monitoring and control (e.g., LiDAR data, ACC commands).
    - **Bandwidth:** High bandwidth required for LiDAR point cloud data (e.g., 10s of Mbps for high-resolution scans).
    - **Latency:** Low latency critical for control loops (e.g., <100ms for ACC commands), but exact requirements are not specified.
    - No explicit details on QoS (Quality of Service) for UDP or ROS2.

  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly mentioned. No details on failover mechanisms, redundant paths, or error recovery.
    - ROS2 supports fault-tolerant communication, but specifics are not provided.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - Not explicitly mentioned. No details on encryption, access control, or network security measures.
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Deployment:** Implied by real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, ACC control) and on-board visualization (Fig. 3).
    - No explicit mention of cloud hosting, but Wi-Fi connectivity suggests potential cloud integration for storage/reporting.

  - **Computational and Storage Requirements:**
    - **Computational:** High-performance requirements for:
      - Point cloud processing (clustering, tracking).
      - Real-time visualization (3D rendering).
      - ACC control logic (path planning, object avoidance).
    - **Storage:** Likely distributed across:
      - On-board storage for real-time data (e.g., LiDAR buffers).
      - Centralized storage for reports and historical data (via **Data Storage FE**).

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly mentioned. No details on horizontal scaling (e.g., adding nodes) or dynamic resource allocation.

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-Premise/Edge:** Primary model for real-time control and processing.
    - Potential hybrid model (edge + cloud) for storage/reporting, but not detailed.

  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - Not explicitly mentioned. No details on uptime guarantees, backup systems, or disaster recovery.
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard for Digital Twin frameworks in manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based architecture** with standardized roles for **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities**. The standard enforces **interoperability** through modular **Functional Entities (FEs)** and does not mandate specific technologies (e.g., **OPC UA** is cited as a potential choice for implementation). **Compliance frameworks** are implied by adherence to ISO 23247, ensuring alignment with manufacturing digital twin best practices. No explicit **certification requirements** or additional **industry specifications** (e.g., IEC 62443 for cybersecurity) are mentioned. The architecture prioritizes **information exchange** and **data consistency** between physical and digital representations, with no detailed **interoperability standards** beyond the ISO framework."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for functional safety in road vehicles, which defines a **risk-based approach** for safety-critical systems, including **automotive Digital Twin applications**. Key measures include:
  - **Cybersecurity protocols**: Integration of **authentication systems** and **access control mechanisms** to mitigate unauthorized access, aligned with **ISO 26262** safety integrity levels (ASIL) for critical components.
  - **Fail-safe mechanisms**: Implementation of **redundant safety architectures** and **watchdog timers** to ensure system resilience against hardware/software failures, as required by **ISO 26262** for **safety-critical functions** (e.g., adaptive cruise control systems per **ISO 15622:2018**).
  - **Threat mitigation strategies**: Compliance with **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)** for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services**, mandating **end-to-end security** (e.g., **authentication, encryption, and secure message exchange**) to prevent spoofing, replay attacks, and data tampering in **interactive Digital Twin cockpits** (as referenced in **Dalibor et al., 2020**).
  - **Risk assessment approaches**: Use of **hazard analysis and risk assessment (HARA)** per **ISO 26262** to classify safety goals and allocate **ASIL levels** (A-D) to system components, ensuring traceability from requirements to implementation.
  - **Safety-critical system considerations**: For **adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems**, **ISO 15622:2018** specifies **performance requirements** (e.g., **time-to-collision mitigation**) and **test procedures** to validate safety under dynamic conditions, directly applicable to Digital Twin simulations.
  - **Model-driven safety**: The **model-driven architecture (MDA)** proposed in **Dalibor et al. (2020)** for **interactive Digital Twin cockpits** emphasizes **formal verification** of safety-critical behaviors using **UML/SysML models**, ensuring compliance with **ISO 26262** through **automated code generation** and **runtime monitoring**."
]
